sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image after SPACE:
- Red rect: [N, O, c, M] - the 'c' dot at col 34 is still unpainted (charcoal)
- Green rect: [R, p, b, Y]
- Bottom: all gray (consumed)
- SPACE did not complete the level
The 'c' (charcoal) dot at red rect col 34 is still unpainted. This is the dot that's behind/under the green T shape. I need to paint this one too. But all bottom colors are consumed (gray).
Wait - maybe I need to use SPACE differently. In level 1, SPACE submitted the solution. Here SPACE didn't work. Maybe SPACE has a different function in level 2, or maybe I need to paint the 'c' dot first.
Looking at the bottom row - all squares are gray now. No colors left to select. But the 'c' dot is still unpainted. 
Maybe the 'c' dot is special - it's under the green T shape, so maybe it should be painted green (N)? Or maybe I need to use SPACE to shift/reset colors?
Let me try pressing SPACE again to see if it shifts the bottom colors like in level 1, or try ACTION7.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully - the bottom row squares are all gray. In level 1, SPACE shifted the bottom row left. Maybe here SPACE does something similar to bring back colors?
Let me try SPACE a few times to see if colors reappear, or try ACTION7.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# All dots painted except the 'c' (charcoal) one at red rect col 34
# Bottom colors all consumed (gray)
# SPACE didn't complete the level
# Need to figure out how to paint the 'c' dot

# Try SPACE to see if it shifts colors back
action(['SPACE'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Bottom after SPACE:")
bottom = []
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if 54 <= n['boundary'][0][0] <= 63 and n['pixels'] == 16:
        bottom.append((n['color'], n['boundary'][0][1]))
bottom.sort(key=lambda x: x[1])
print(f"  {bottom}")

print("Dots:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 4 and n['boundary'][0][0] < 50:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, row={n['boundary'][0][0]}, col={n['boundary'][0][1]}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"SPACE": "Submits solution or shifts colors", "MOUSE": "Select bottom color, click dot to paint it"}, "object_taxonomy": {"top_panel": "Target pattern [O,p,R,b,N,Y,M]", "red_rect": "4 dots at row 22", "green_rect": "4 dots at row 36", "bottom_panel": "Paint palette"}, "hud_map": {"timer_bar": "row ~53"}, "win_pattern": {"paint_dots_then_space": "Paint all dots, SPACE to submit"}, "level_log": {"level_1": "Paint 4 dots, SPACE to submit"}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 2: All dots painted except 'c' dot at red rect col 34. Bottom colors consumed. SPACE didn't complete.", "goal_model": "Paint all dots including the charcoal one, then submit", "recent_findings": ["Charcoal dot under green T not painted", "SPACE didn't submit - maybe need all dots painted first"], "open_questions": ["How to paint the charcoal dot?", "Does SPACE shift colors?"], "current_plan": "Try SPACE to shift colors, then paint the charcoal dot", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
